title: Hydrotrida caroliniana
taxonomy: Hydrotrida caroliniana is classified as a species of bryozoan, which is a type of marine invertebrate animal. It belongs to the class Stenolaemata and is characterized by its small, colonial structure. The class Stenolaemata is a part of the larger phylum Bryozoa, which contains a diverse range of bryozoan species. Within the phylum Bryozoa, the class Stenolaemata is further divided into several orders, with Hydrotrida caroliniana belonging to one of these orders. Further classification within the order is needed to provide a more specific taxonomic classification for this species.
uses: Hydrotrida caroliniana, also known as the Carolina springbell or springbell, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial for reducing swelling and pain.
